name: Modular flavor symmetry
summary: Yukawa couplings are modular forms of weight k under a finite modular group Gamma_N (e.g., A_4, S_4, A_5). The single complex modulus tau provides a small expansion parameter; modular weights play the role of FN charges but are constrained by modular invariance, producing predictive Yukawa structures with fewer free parameters than abelian Froggatt-Nielsen.

addresses_puzzles.note: Single modulus tau replaces multiple flavon VEVs; modular forms of definite weight constrain Yukawa structure tightly.
Modular A_4 / S_4 models reproduce PMNS angles and provide testable predictions for the CP phase and neutrino mass ordering.

Specific modular weights and SL(2,Z) representations can yield charged-lepton mass ratios close to the Koide value (Q = 2/3); the formula appears as an approximate consequence rather than an exact prediction (Feruglio 2019, Penedo-Petcov 2019).
Yukawa entries are modular forms of a finite modular group at fixed weight; the number of free parameters drops from O(20) to O(few) (Feruglio 2017; Ding-King 2024 review).

new_symmetries: finite modular group Gamma_N (A_4, S_4, A_5, ...)
introduces.note: Modulus VEV (Re tau, Im tau), overall Yukawa scale; modular forms then largely fix the structure.

history: Feruglio (2017) introduced modular flavor symmetries from string-inspired considerations. Extensive systematic studies of A_4, S_4, A_5 modular models for both quark and lepton sectors followed (Kobayashi-Nilles-Saga-Tanimoto 2018-2020).

references.citation: Feruglio, 'Are neutrino masses modular forms?', in From My Vast Repertoire (2019)
Kobayashi, Tanaka, Tatsuishi, 'Neutrino mixing from finite modular groups', Phys. Rev. D 98, 016004 (2018)
